verses.sanskrit: तपस्स्वाध्याय निरतं तपस्वी वाग्विदां वरम् ।नारदं परिपप्रच्छ वाल्मीकिर्मुनिपुंगवम् ॥१-१-१॥
verses.text: The ascetic Valmiki inquired Naarada - 'who engaged deeply in austerity and study of Vedas, best among the eloquent and eminent among sages' (as follows:). [1-1-1]

verses.sanskrit: कोन्वस्मिन् साम्प्रतं लोके गुणवान् कश्च वीर्यवान् ।धर्मज्ञश्च कृतज्ञश्च सत्यवाक्यो धृढव्रतः ॥१-१-२॥
verses.text:
"Who really is that person in this present world, who is virtuous and vigorous, a conscientious 
	one, one who is mindful of good deeds done to him, and also a speaker of truth and who is determined in his deed... [1-1-2]

verses.sanskrit: चारित्रेण च को युक्तः सर्वभूतेषु को हितः ।विद्वान् कः कः समर्थश्च कश्च एक प्रियदर्शनः ॥१-१-३॥
verses.text:
"Who is appropriate in disposition... who is interested in welfare of all beings... who is 
	adept and also an able one... also uniquely pleasant to look at... [1-1-3]

verses.sanskrit: आत्मवान् को जित क्रोधो द्युतिमान् कः अनसूयकः ।कस्य बिभ्यति देवाः च जात रोषस्य संयुगे ॥१-१-४॥
verses.text:
"Who is that self-composed one, who controlled his ire, who is brilliant, non-jealous and whom do even
	the gods fear, when provoked to war... [1-1-4]

verses.sanskrit: एतत् इच्छामि अहम् श्रोतुम् परम् कौतूहलम् हि मे ।महर्षे त्वम् समर्थोऽसि ज्ञातुम् एवम् विधम् नरम् ॥१-१-५॥
verses.text:
"All this I wish to listen from you, oh! Great Sage, you are competent to know this kind of man, 
and indeed my inquisitiveness is immense..." Thus Valmiki enquired with Narada. [1-1-5]

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा च एतत् त्रिलोकज्ञो वाल्मीकेः नारदो वचः ।श्रूयताम् इति च आमंत्र्य प्रहृष्टो वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् ॥१-१-६॥
verses.text:
On listening all those words of Valmiki, Narada, the knower of all the three worlds, said "let 
it be heard..." and beckoning at Sage Valmiki to listen attentively, he spoke these words very gladly. 
[1-1-6]

verses.sanskrit: बहवो दुर्लभाः च एव ये त्वया कीर्तिता गुणाः ।मुने वक्ष्ष्यामि अहम् बुद्ध्वा तैः उक्तः श्रूयताम् नरः ॥१-१-७॥
verses.text: "Oh! Sage Valmiki, the merits which you have extolled are many, and unattainable. I, after having known from Brahma of such a man with all these merits, will speak on...Let it be heard." Thus Narada started to say. [1-1-7]

verses.sanskrit: इक्ष्वाकु वंश प्रभवो रामो नाम जनैः श्रुतः ।नियत आत्मा महावीर्यो द्युतिमान् धृतिमान् वशी ॥१-१-८॥
verses.text:
"One emerged from Ikshvaku dynasty, known to people as Rama by name. He is with a controlled self, 
highly valorous, resplendent, steadfast and a controller of (vice and vile... or) his own senses. [1-1-8]

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धिमान् नीतिमान् वाङ्ग्मी श्रीमान् शत्रु निबर्हणः ।विपुलांसो महाबाहुः कंबु ग्रीवो महाहनुः ॥१-१-९॥
verses.text:
"He is a wise one, moralist, eloquent, glorious, and a destroyer of enemies. His arms are lengthy, 
with three lines in the neck [indicative of good fortune], and cheekbones high... [1-1-9]

verses.sanskrit: महोरस्को महेष्वासो गूढ जत्रुः अरिन्दमः ।आजानु बाहुः सुशिराः सुललाटः सुविक्रमः ॥१-१-१०॥
verses.text:
"He is lion-chested, with concealed collarbones, knee-length are his arms, and his is a long bow, an enemy-subjugator, 
and his emperor's countenance is with a crowning-head and an ample forehead, and his gait is majestic like that of a lion... 
[1-1-10]

verses.sanskrit: समः सम विभक्त अंगः स्निग्ध वर्णः प्रतापवान् ।पीन वक्षा विशालाक्षो लक्ष्मीवान् शुभ लक्षणः ॥१-१-११॥
verses.text:
"He is built proportionately physically, with limbs poised symmetrically, sinew-chested, wide-eyed, complexioned 
charmingly... he is a prosperous one with all the auspicious features ... 
[1-1-11]

verses.sanskrit: धर्मज्ञः सत्यसन्धः च प्रजानाम् च हिते रतः ।यशस्वी ज्ञानसंपन्नः शुचिः वश्यः समाधिमान् ॥१-१-१२॥
verses.text:
"He is the knower of rectitude, bidden by the truth, also his concern is in the welfare of subjects, 
proficient in prudence, clean in his conduct, self-controlled and an attentive one... 
[1-1-12]

verses.sanskrit: प्रजापति समः श्रीमान् धता रिपु निषूदनः ।रक्षिता जीवलोकस्य धर्मस्य परि रक्षिता॥१-१-१३॥
verses.text:
"He equals Lord Vishnu, the Lord of all creatures, Sustainer of the Universe, together with Sri Lakshmi (shriimaan = lakShmiivaan), Eliminator of enemies,
 Protector of the world of living creatures and complete Guardian of probity... 
[1-1-13]

verses.sanskrit: रक्षिता स्वस्य धर्मस्य स्व जनस्य च रक्षिता ।वेद वेदाङ्ग तत्त्वज्ञो धनुर् वेदे च निष्ठितः ॥१-१-१४॥
verses.text:
"He is the preserver of his own righteousness and also a champion for adherent's welfare in the same 
righteousness, and he is a scholar in the essence of Veda-s and their ancillaries, too. He is an expert 
in dhanurVeda , the Art of Archery... [1-1-14]

verses.sanskrit: सर्व शास्त्र अर्थ तत्त्वज्ञो स्मृतिमान् प्रतिभानवान् ।सर्वलोक प्रियः साधुः अदीनाअत्मा विचक्षणः ॥१-१-१५॥
verses.text:
"He is the knower of the meaning and essence of all the scriptures, excellent at memory, a brilliant one, 
and dear to all the worlds, gentle, level-headed and clear-headed in discriminating and distinguishing... 
[1-1-15]

verses.sanskrit: सर्वदा अभिगतः सद्भिः समुद्र इव सिन्धुभिः ।अर्यः सर्वसमः च एव सदैव प्रिय दर्शनः ॥१-१-१६॥
verses.text:
"Like an ocean that is reached by rivers, that reverential one too is always accessible 
and reachable by clean-minded ones, and he treats all equally, and ever a feast to eye... [1-1-16]

verses.sanskrit: स च सर्व गुणोपेतः कौसल्य आनंद वर्धनः ।समुद्र इव गाम्भीर्ये धैर्येण हिमवान् इव ॥१-१-१७॥
verses.text:
"He who betters the happiness of his mother Kausalya is an embodiment of all noble merits, and in profundity 
he is like an unfathomable ocean, and by fortitude he is unalterable like the kingly Himalayan mountain... 
[1-1-17]

verses.sanskrit: विष्णुना सदृशो वीर्ये सोमवत् प्रिय दर्शनः ।काल अग्नि सदृशः क्रोधे क्षमया पृथ्वी समः ॥१-१-१८॥
verses.text:
"In valour Rama is comparable to Vishnu, and in his looks he is attractive like the moon, he equals 
the earth in his forgiveness, but he is matchable to world ending fire in his wrath... and in benevolence 
he is identical to Kubera, God of Wealth, and in his candour he is like Dharma itself, the 
Truth personified, with none to rival him... [1-1-18, 19a]

verses.sanskrit: धनदेन समः त्यागे सत्ये धर्म इव अपरः ।तम् एवम् गुण संपन्नम् रामम् सत्य पराक्रमम् ॥१-१-१९॥
verses.text:
"Lord of the land King Dasaratha with an intent for doing good to the people, affectionately desired to endow with regent-hood to that Rama, 
the eldest and a dear son to Dasaratha and who possessed such merits with true valour, together with best of qualities, 
and concerned for welfare of the people....[1-1-29b, 20, 21a]

verses.sanskrit: ज्येष्टम् श्रेष्ट गुणैः युक्तम् प्रियम् दशरथः सुतम् ।प्रकृतीनाम् हितैः युक्तम् प्रकृति प्रिय काम्यया ॥१-१-२०॥
यौव राज्येन संयोक्तुम् ऐच्छत् प्रीत्या महीपतिः ।तस्य अभिषेक संभारान् दृष्ट्वा भार्या अथ कैकयी ॥१-१-२१॥
verses.text:
"Then seeing the arrangements for the anointment of Rama as crown-prince, Kaikeyi, the dear wife 
and a queen of Dasaratha, solicited boons that were once accorded to her by Dasharatha, for the 
banishment of Rama and anointment of Bharata as boons. [1-1-21b, 22]

verses.sanskrit: पूर्वम् दत्त वरा देवी वरम् एनम् अयाचत ।विवासनम् च रामस्य भरतस्य अभिषेचनम् ॥१-१-२२॥
स सत्य वचनात् राजा धर्म पाशेन संयतः ।विवासयामास सुतम् रामम् दशरथः प्रियम् ॥१-१-२३॥
verses.text:
"Bound by his true plighted word and by the noose of righteousness that king Dasharatha 
		exiled his dear son Rama to forests. [1-1-23]

verses.sanskrit: स जगाम वनम् वीरः प्रतिज्ञाम् अनुपालयन् ।पितुर् वचन निर्देशात् कैकेय्याः प्रिय कारणात् ॥१-१-२४॥
verses.text:
"That warrior Rama went to forest for pleasing Kaikeyi, and as 
directed by his father, and to follow his father's word of honour. [1-1-24]

verses.sanskrit: तम् व्रजंतम् प्रियो भ्राता लक्ष्मणः अनुजगाम ह ।स्नेहात् विनय संपन्नः सुमित्र आनंद वर्धनः ॥१-१-२५॥
verses.text:
"While that Rama was going on exile, Lakshmana, endowed with humbleness, and augmentor of his mother Sumitra's joy, and a loving and a 
dear brother to Rama, followed him out of affection, showing brotherly fraternity.[1-1-25, 26a]

verses.sanskrit: भ्रातरम् दयितो भ्रातुः सौभ्रात्रम् अनु दर्शयन् ।रामस्य दयिता भार्या नित्यम् प्राण समा हिता ॥१-१-२६॥
verses.text:
"Even Seetha, daughter-in-law of Dasaratha, and Rama's beloved wife equalling to Rama's life, ever well disposed, born in the family of Janaka and one as though 
	fashioned by a Divine marvel, endowed with all good characteristics and the best among women, followed Rama to forest, like Rohini following the Moon... [1-1-26b, 
	27, 28a]

verses.sanskrit: जनकस्य कुले जाता देव मायेव निर्मिता ।सर्व लक्षण संपन्ना नारीणाम् उत्तमा वधूः ॥१-१-२७॥
सीताप्य अनुगता रामम् शशिनम् रोहिणी यथा ।पौरैः अनुगतो दूरम् पित्रा दशरथेन च ॥१-१-२८॥
verses.text:
"The citizens of Ayodhya and his father Dasaratha followed virtuous Rama for a 
distance when he started on his exile. Later Rama reached the tribal chief named Guha, who liked 
Rama, on the bank of River Ganga in a town called Sringaberapura. Together with Guha, 
Lakshmana and Seetha, he left off the charioteer. [1-1-28b, 29, 30a]

verses.sanskrit: शृन्गिबेर पुरे सूतम् गंगा कूले व्यसर्जयत् ।गुहम् आसाद्य धर्मात्मा निषाद अधिपतिम् प्रियम् ॥१-१-२९॥
गुहेअन सहितो रामो लक्ष्मणेन च सीतया ।ते वनेन वनम् गत्वा नदीः तीर्त्वा बहु उदकाः ॥१-१-३०॥
verses.text:
"That trio of Seetha, Rama and Lakshmana after going from forest to forest and crossing many a river with much waters, 
by the ordainment of Bharadwaja, arrived at Chitrakuta, and building a handsome cottage there, they lived happily and delightfully 
in the woods there, like gods and gandharvas... [1-1-30b, 31, 32a]

verses.sanskrit: चित्रकूटम् अनुप्राप्य भरद्वाजस्य शासनात् ।रम्यम् आवसथम् कृत्वा रममाणा वने त्रयः ॥१-१-३१॥
देव गन्धर्व संकाशाः तत्र ते न्यवसन् सुखम् ।चित्रकूटम् गते रामे पुत्र शोक आतुरः तथा ॥१-१-३२॥
verses.text:
"On Rama going to Chitrakuta thus, King Dasharatha tormented by the grief for son, went to heavens 
weeping for son... [1-1-32b, 33a]

verses.sanskrit: राजा दशरथः स्वर्गम् जगाम विलपन् सुतम् ।गते तु तस्मिन् भरतो वसिष्ठ प्रमुखैः द्विजैः ॥१-१-३३॥
verses.text:
"On Dasharatha's dying, though Bharata had been appointed for kingship by Sage Vashishta and other Brahmanas, that highly mighty one did not desire kingdom.
	That warrior Bharata went to forest to propitiate the venerable Rama... [1-1-33b. 34]

verses.sanskrit: नियुज्यमानो राज्याय न इच्छत् राज्यम् महाबलः ।स जगाम वनम् वीरो राम पाद प्रसादकः ॥१-१-३४॥
गत्वा तु स महात्मानम् रामम् सत्य पराक्रमम् ।अयाचत् भ्रातरम् रामम् आर्य भाव पुरस्कृतः ॥१-१-३५॥
verses.text:
"That Bharata, possessed of a worthy way of thinking [to get Rama installed as king], on going to Rama, begged his brother Rama, a great souled one and a 
	truly courageous one. Bharata spoke words in this manner to Rama - "You alone, the knower of probity, be the king." ... ' [1-1-35, 36a]

verses.sanskrit: त्वम् एव राजा धर्मज्ञ इति रामम् वचः अब्रवीत् ।रामोऽपि परमोदारः सुमुखः सुमहायशाः ॥१-१-३६॥
verses.text: "Even though Rama was very benevolent, gracious also, highly famed and with great prowess, Rama did not desire kingdom due to father's injunction... [1-1-36b, 37a]

verses.sanskrit: न च इच्छत् पितुर् आदेशात् राज्यम् रामो महाबलः ।पादुके च अस्य राज्याय न्यासम् दत्त्वा पुनः पुनः ॥१-१-३७॥
verses.text:
"Thereafter, repeatedly turning Bharata back, the elder brother, Rama, gave his wooden shoes to Bharata and entrusting them to represent him 
for the sake of kingdom. [1-1-37b, 38a]

verses.sanskrit: निवर्तयामास ततो भरतम् भरत अग्रजः ।स कामम् अनवाप्य एव राम पादा उपस्पृशन् ॥१-१-३८॥
verses.text:
"That Bharata, thus with a desire unfulfilled, ruled the kingdom from Nandigrama, touching Rama's sandals representing feet of Rama, with an
expectation of Rama's arrival... [1-1-38b, 39a]

verses.sanskrit: नन्दि ग्रामे अकरोत् राज्यम् राम आगमन कांक्षया ।गते तु भरते श्रीमान् सत्य सन्धो जितेन्द्रियः ॥१-१-३९॥
verses.text:
"On the departure of Bharata, that splendid Rama, truth-bound, and self-controlled, anticipated the repeated 
visits of citizens and other people. To be undisturbed, Rama then entered Dandaka forests... [1-1-39b, 40]

verses.sanskrit: रामः तु पुनः आलक्ष्य नागरस्य जनस्य च ।तत्र आगमनम् एकाग्रो दण्डकान् प्रविवेश ह ॥१-१-४०॥
प्रविश्य तु महाअरण्यम् रामो राजीव लोचनः ।विराधम् राक्षसम् हत्वा शरभंगम् ददर्श ह ॥१-१-४१॥
verses.text:
"That lotus-eyed Rama, on his entering the vast Dandaka forest, killed the demon Viraadha, and 
indeed beheld Sage Sharabhanga and also Sage Suteekhsna, Sage Agastya and likewise Sage Agastya's 
brother... [1-1-41, 42a]

verses.sanskrit: सुतीक्ष्णम् च अपि अगस्त्यम् च अगस्त्य भ्रातरम् तथा ।अगस्त्य वचनात् च एव जग्राह ऐन्द्रम् शरासनम् ॥१-१-४२॥
verses.text:
"On Sage Agastya's word, Rama, with great delight, received from Agastya, Indra's bow along with the sword and two quivers with ever replenishing
 arrows... [1-1-42b, 43a]

verses.sanskrit: खड्गम् च परम प्रीतः तूणी च अक्षय सायकौ ।वसतः तस्य रामस्य वने वन चरैः सह ॥१-१-४३॥
verses.text:
"During Rama's stay in the forest, all the sages together with other forest dwellers,
approached him for the elimination of ogres and demons... [1-1-43b, 44a]

verses.sanskrit: ऋषयः अभ्यागमन् सर्वे वधाय असुर रक्षसाम् ।स तेषाम् प्रति शुश्राव राक्षसानाम् तथा वने ॥१-१-४४॥
verses.text:
"He [Rama] agreed to that supplication of those sages dwelling in the forest of demons. A promise was also made by Rama to the sages who equalled 
the fire and who were living in that Dandaka forest about killing of the demons in combat... [1-1-44b, 45]

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिज्ञातः च रामेण वधः संयति रक्षसाम् ।ऋषीणाम् अग्नि कल्पानाम् दंडकारण्य वासीनाम् ॥१-१-४५॥
तेन तत्र एव वसता जनस्थान निवासिनी ।विरूपिता शूर्पणखा राक्षसी काम रूपिणी ॥१-१-४६॥
verses.text:
"While Rama was living there in the Dandaka forest, a guise-changing demoness named Shuurpa-nakha, who was 
a resident of Janasthana, a portion of Dandaka forest, was disfigured by him... [1-1-46]

verses.sanskrit: ततः शूर्पणखा वाक्यात् उद्युक्तान् सर्व राक्षसान् ।खरम् त्रिशिरसम् च एव दूषणम् च एव राक्षसम् ॥१-१-४७॥
verses.text:
"Then in a battle, Rama killed all demons who became zealously active incited by Suurphanakha's words, 
including the demons Khara, Trishira, Duushana together with all of their foot-followers... [1-1-47, 48a]

verses.sanskrit: निजघान रणे रामः तेषाम् च एव पद अनुगान् ।वने तस्मिन् निवसता जनस्थान निवासिनाम् ॥१-१-४८॥
verses.text: "During Rama's stay in that forest, fourteen thousand demon inhabitants of Janasthana became slain... [1-1-48b, 49a]

verses.sanskrit: रक्षसाम् निहतानि असन् सहस्राणि चतुर् दश ।ततो ज्ञाति वधम् श्रुत्वा रावणः क्रोध मूर्छितः ॥१-१-४९॥
verses.text:
"Then, on hearing the slaughter of his kinsmen, Ravana convulsed in anger and sought to get the help of a 
		demon named Maareecha... [1-1-49b, 50a]

verses.sanskrit: सहायम् वरयामास मारीचम् नाम राक्षसम् ।वार्यमाणः सुबहुशो मारीचेन स रावणः ॥१-१-५०॥
verses.text:
"But Maareecha restrained Ravana telling him many times, 'Oh, Ravana! Rivarly with that formidable 
Rama is not appropriate of you...' [1-1-50b, 51a]

verses.sanskrit: न विरोधो बलवता क्षमो रावण तेन ते ।अनादृत्य तु तत् वाक्यम् रावणः काल चोदितः ॥१-१-५१॥
verses.text:
"Then disregarding Maareecha's words and impelled by his own fate, Ravana advanced to the 
place of Rama's hermitage along with Maareecha... [1-1-51b, 52a]

verses.sanskrit: जगाम सह मारीचः तस्य आश्रम पदम् तदा ।तेन मायाविना दूरम् अपवाह्य नृप आत्मजौ ॥१-१-५२॥
verses.text: "After taking away of Rama and Lakshmana to a distance by the trickster Maareecha, Ravana stole Rama's wife after killing Jatayu... [1-1-52b, 53a]

verses.sanskrit: जहार भार्याम् रामस्य गृध्रम् हत्वा जटायुषम् ।गृध्रम् च निहतम् दृष्ट्वा हृताम् श्रुत्वा च मैथिलीम् ॥१-१-५३॥
verses.text:
"Raghava on seeing the smitten Jatayu and also on hearing about Seetha's abduction, wailed with disturbed senses, 
seething with anguish  ... [1-1-53b, 54a]

verses.sanskrit: राघवः शोक संतप्तो विललाप आकुल इन्द्रियः ।ततः तेन एव शोकेन गृध्रम् दग्ध्वा जटायुषम् ॥१-१-५४॥
verses.text:
"Rama then cremated that vulture Jataayu with that state of anguish, and while searching for Seetha in forest, he 
indeed saw a demon named Kabandha, deformed in his looks and with a terrible appearance... [1-1-54b, 
55]

verses.sanskrit: मार्गमाणो वने सीताम् राक्षसम् संददर्श ह ।कबंधम् नाम रूपेण विकृतम् घोर दर्शनम् ॥१-१-५५॥
तम् निहत्य महाबाहुः ददाह स्वर्गतः च सः ।स च अस्य कथयामास शबरीम् धर्म चारिणीम् ॥१-१-५६॥
verses.text:
"That Rama with great arms after slaying Kabandha, cremated him and Kabandha obtanined heaven. Before going to heaven, he spoke thus to Rama:
'Oh Raghava! You proceed to Sabari, a lady with righteous conduct, an expert in probity and a devotee.'... [1-1-56, 57a]

verses.sanskrit: श्रमणाम् धर्म निपुणाम् अभिगच्छ इति राघव ।सः अभ्य गच्छन् महातेजाः शबरीम् शत्रु सूदनः ॥१-१-५७॥
verses.text: "That great resplendent Rama, the son of Dasaratha, an anhillator of enemies, approached Sabari and was thoroughly honoured by her... [1-1-57b, 58a]

verses.sanskrit: शबर्या पूजितः सम्यक् रामो दशरथ आत्मजः ।पंपा तीरे हनुमता संगतो वानरेण ह ॥१-१-५८॥
verses.text:
"Rama was united indeed with the Vanara Hanuma on the banks of Lake Pampa, and upon the word of Hanuma, Rama met with 
	Sugreeva... [1-1-58b, 59b]

verses.sanskrit: हनुमत् वचनात् च एव सुग्रीवेण समागतः ।सुग्रीवाय च तत् सर्वम् शंसत् रामो महाबलः ॥१-१-५९॥
verses.text:
"The highly powerful Rama detailed from the beginning all that happened, and in particular the abduction of Seetha, to Sugriva 
and to Hanuma also... [1-1-59b, 60a]

verses.sanskrit: आदितः तत् यथा वृत्तम् सीतायाः च विशेषतः ।सुग्रीवः च अपि तत् सर्वम् श्रुत्वा रामस्य वानरः ॥१-१-६०॥
verses.text: "The Vanara Sugreeva after listening to all that talk of Rama made friendship with Rama delightedly with sacred fire as witness... [1-1-60b, 61a]

verses.sanskrit: चकार सख्यम् रामेण प्रीतः च एव अग्नि साक्षिकम् ।ततो वानर राजेन वैर अनुकथनम् प्रति ॥१-१-६१॥
verses.text:
"Then out of affection, the incident was related with regard to Sugreeva's enemity with Vali  
in its entirety by the Vanara King with grief to Rama ... [1-1-61b, 62a]

verses.sanskrit: रामाय आवेदितम् सर्वम् प्रणयात् दुःखितेन च ।प्रतिज्ञातम् च रामेण तदा वालि वधम् प्रति ॥१-१-६२॥
verses.text: "Then a solemn promise was made by Rama with regards to killing of Vali. In that regard Vanara Sugreeva also described Vali's strength... [1-1-62b, 63a]

verses.sanskrit: वालिनः च बलम् तत्र कथयामास वानरः ।सुग्रीवः शंकितः च आसीत् नित्यम् वीर्येण राघवे ॥१-१-६३॥
verses.text:
"But Sugreeva remained ever doubtful with respect to Raghava, about Raghava's prowess. Sugreeva showed Dundubhi's big and great mountain-like
dead body to gain confidence in Raghava... [1-1-63b, 64]

verses.sanskrit: राघवः प्रत्ययार्थम् तु दुंदुभेः कायम् उत्तमम् ।दर्शयामास सुग्रीवः महापर्वत संनिभम् ॥१-१-६४॥
उत्स्मयित्वा महाबाहुः प्रेक्ष्य च अस्ति महाबलः ।पाद अंगुष्टेन चिक्षेप संपूर्णम् दश योजनम् ॥१-१-६५॥
verses.text:
Great armed and very powerful Rama after seeing the bones of Dundubhi smiled and by the big toe of his foot threw it wholly for ten 
yojana-s length. [1-1-65]

verses.sanskrit: बिभेद च पुनः सालान् सप्त एकेन महा इषुणा ।गिरिम् रसातलम् चैव जनयन् प्रत्ययम् तथा ॥१-१-६६॥
verses.text:
"Then again Rama ripped through seven massive Sala trees with only one great arrow, which not only went through 
the trees but also rent through a mountain, and to the nethermost subterranean of earth, in order to 
create faith in the mind of Sugreeva... [1-1-66]

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रीत मनाः तेन विश्वस्तः स महाकपिः ।किष्किंधाम् राम सहितो जगाम च गुहाम् तदा ॥१-१-६७॥
verses.text:
"Thereafter great monkey Sugreeva with a gladdened heart by that act of Rama, proceeded to cave-like Kishkindha then along with Rama 
and with confidence in Rama's strength... 
[1-1-67]

verses.sanskrit: ततः अगर्जत् हरिवरः सुग्रीवो हेम पिंगलः ।तेन नादेन महता निर्जगाम हरीश्वरः ॥१-१-६८॥
verses.text:
"Then that best monkey Sugreeva, with a golden hue, made a roaring sound. By that loud sound, the king of monkeys, Vali, emerged out... 
[1-1-68]

verses.sanskrit: अनुमान्य तदा ताराम् सुग्रीवेण समागतः ।निजघान च तत्र एनम् शरेण एकेन राघवः ॥१-१-६९॥
verses.text: "Then after Tara [wife of Vali] permitting, Vali met with Sugreeva in a combat. And Rama killed this Vali with a single arrow there... [1-1-69]

verses.sanskrit: ततः सुग्रीव वचनात् हत्वा वालिनम् आहवे ।सुग्रीवम् एव तत् राज्ये राघवः प्रत्यपादयत् ॥१-१-७०॥
verses.text: "Then Raghava after killing Vali in a combat as per Sugreeva's plea, installed Sugreeva alone as the king of Vanaras.. [1-1-70]

verses.sanskrit: स च सर्वान् समानीय वानरान् वानरर्षभः ।दिशः प्रस्थापयामास दिदृक्षुः जनक आत्मजाम् ॥१-१-७१॥
verses.text: "That best among monkeys, Sugreeva, after summoning all Vanaras, dispatched them in different directions to behold the daughter of Janaka... [1-1-71]

verses.sanskrit: ततो गृध्रस्य वचनात् संपातेः हनुमान् बली ।शत योजन विस्तीर्णम् पुप्लुवे लवण अर्णवम् ॥१-१-७२॥
verses.text:
"After that, the strong Hanuma, upon the word of the vulture Sampati who was elder brother of Jatayu, flew across the salty ocean
 one hundred yojanas in breadth... [1-1-72]

verses.sanskrit: तत्र लंकाम् समासाद्य पुरीम् रावण पालिताम् ।ददर्श सीताम् ध्यायन्तीम् अशोक वनिकाम् गताम् ॥१-१-७३॥
verses.text: "On reaching the city of Lanka ruled by Ravana, Hanuma saw Seetha situated in Ashoka gardens there and meditating on Rama alone... [1-1-73]

verses.sanskrit: निवेदयित्वा अभिज्ञानम् प्रवृत्तिम् च निवेद्य च ।समाश्वास्य च वैदेहीम् मर्दयामास तोरणम् ॥१-१-७४॥
verses.text:
"After delivering to Seetha the mark of identification in the form of a ring from Rama and also communicating
 Rama's disposition and consoling Vaidehi, Hanuma destroyed the welcome-arch of Ashoka gardens... [1-1-74]

verses.sanskrit: पंच सेन अग्रगान् हत्वा सप्त मंत्रि सुतान् अपि ।शूरम् अक्षम् च निष्पिष्य ग्रहणम् समुपागमत् ॥१-१-७५॥
verses.text: "After killing five army chiefs and seven minister's sons and crushing the valiant Aksha, Hanuma got into state of captivity... [1-1-75]

verses.sanskrit: अस्त्रेण उन्मुक्तम् आत्मानम् ज्ञात्वा पैतामहात् वरात् ।मर्षयन् राक्षसान् वीरो यन्त्रिणः तान् यदृच्छया ॥१-१-७६॥
verses.text:
"Knowing himself to be free from the captivity of the weapon according to Brahma's boon, the valiant great monkey Hanuma
enduring those demons with self-will who tied his tail with ropes with the intention of burning it, there after burning the city of Lanka while 
 leaving from fire the Seetha of Mithila, got back again to convey the pleasant news to Rama ... [1-1-76, 77]

verses.sanskrit: ततो दग्ध्वा पुरीम् लंकाम् ऋते सीताम् च मैथिलीम् ।रामाय प्रियम् आख्यातुम् पुनः आयात् महाकपिः ॥१-१-७७॥
सः अभिगम्य महात्मानम् कृत्वा रामम् प्रदक्षिणम् ।न्यवेदयत् अमेयात्मा दृष्टा सीता इति तत्त्वतः ॥१-१-७८॥
verses.text:
"Approaching the great Rama and peforming circumambulation around Rama, Hanuma having an inestimable intelligence,
submitted in essence thus: 'Seen Seetha...' [1-1-78]

verses.sanskrit: ततः सुग्रीव सहितो गत्वा तीरम् महा उदधेः ।समुद्रम् क्षोभयामास शरैः आदित्य सन्निभैः ॥१-१-७९॥
verses.text:
"There-after, Rama along with Sugreeva and other monkeys went to the shore of Great Ocean, and put the Ocean-god to turmoil with his arrows 
comparable to the Sun... [1-1-79]

verses.sanskrit: दर्शयामास च आत्मानम् समुद्रः सरिताम् पतिः ।समुद्र वचनात् च एव नलम् सेतुम् अकारयत् ॥१-१-८०॥
verses.text: "The Ocean-god revealed himself and upon his word, Nala built the bridge across the ocean... [1-1-80]

verses.sanskrit: तेन गत्वा पुरीम् लंकाम् हत्वा रावणम् आहवे ।रामः सीताम् अनुप्राप्य पराम् व्रीडाम् उपागमत् ॥१-१-८१॥
verses.text: "Rama, having gone to the city of Lanka by that bridge, killing Ravana in a battle, experienced great shame on getting Seetha back... [1-1-81]

verses.sanskrit: ताम् उवाच ततः रामः परुषम् जन संसदि ।अमृष्यमाणा सा सीता विवेश ज्वलनम् सती ॥१-१-८२॥
verses.text:
"There-after, Rama spoke harshly to Seetha in the assembly of the people. Being devoted to her husband, that Seetha, intolerant of the
 harsh words of Rama, entered into burning fire... [1-1-82]

verses.sanskrit: ततः अग्नि वचनात् सीताम् ज्ञात्वा विगत कल्मषाम् ।कर्मणा तेन महता त्रैलोक्यम् स चराचरम् ॥१-१-८३॥
verses.text:
"Then, upon the word of Fire-god, having known Seetha to be sinless, Rama became extremely glad.
Worshipped by all gods he became resplendent. By that accomplishment of the great Raghava the three worlds together with mobile and immobile
beings, and with groups of gods and sages became exultant... [1-1-83, 84]

verses.sanskrit: स देवर्षि गणम् तुष्टम् राघवस्य महात्मनः ॥बभौ रामः संप्रहृष्टः पूजितः सर्व देवतैः ॥१-१-८४॥
अभ्यषिच्य च लंकायाम् राक्षस इन्द्रम् विभीषणम् ।कृतकृत्यः तदा रामो विज्वरः प्रमुमोद ह ॥१-१-८५॥
verses.text:
"Enthroning Vibheeshana as the chieftain of demons in Lanka, and with a done deed, 
Rama then rejoiced highly indeed free from distress... [1-1-85]

verses.sanskrit: देवताभ्यो वराम् प्राप्य समुत्थाप्य च वानरान् ।अयोध्याम् प्रस्थितः रामः पुष्पकेण सुहृत् वृतः ॥१-१-८६॥
verses.text:
"After obtaining a boon from the gods, Rama  raised up monkeys from death. He then travelled to Ayodhya by Pushpaka aircraft
surrounded by good hearted ones... 
[1-1-86]

verses.sanskrit: भरद्वाज आश्रमम् गत्वा रामः सत्यपराक्रमः ।भरतस्य अंतिकम् रामो हनूमंतम् व्यसर्जयत् ॥१-१-८७॥
verses.text: "Rama, with true valour, on going to sage Bharadwaja's hermitage, sent Hanuma to the presence of Bharata... [1-1-87]

verses.sanskrit: पुनः आख्यायिकाम् जल्पन् सुग्रीव सहितः तदा ।पुष्पकम् तत् समारूह्य नंदिग्रामम् ययौ तदा ॥१-१-८८॥
verses.text:
"Then, having boarded Pushpaka aircraft again after leaving the hermitage of Bharadwaja, and narrating past history
 to Sugreeva and others about the events in the days of his exile in forests, while flying overhead 
of the very same places, Rama went to Nandigrama... [1-1-88]

verses.sanskrit: नंदिग्रामे जटाम् हित्वा भ्रातृभिः सहितो अनघः ।रामः सीताम् अनुप्राप्य राज्यम् पुनः अवाप्तवान् ॥१-१-८९॥
verses.text:
"That sinless Rama rejoining with all of his brothers in the village Nandigrama removed his matted hair. 
Thus he, on regaining Seetha and on discarding hermit's role again became 
a householder, and he regained his kingdom also... [1-1-89]

verses.sanskrit: प्रहृष्टो मुदितो लोकः तुष्टः पुष्टः सुधार्मिकः ।निरामयो हि अरोगः च दुर्भिक्ष भय वर्जितः ॥१-१-९०॥
verses.text:
"When Rama is ruling the world will be highly rejoiced and happy, satisfied and abundant, 
also very righteous, wholesome, disease-free, and free from fear of famine..." Thus Narada is 
foreseeing the future and telling Valmiki. [1-1-90]

verses.sanskrit: न पुत्र मरणम् केचित् द्रक्ष्यन्ति पुरुषाः क्वचित् ।नार्यः च अविधवा नित्यम् भविष्यन्ति पति व्रताः ॥१-१-९१॥
verses.text:
"While Rama is on the throne men will not see the deaths of their children anywhere in their lifetime, 
and the ladies will remain husband-devouted and unwidowed during their lifetime... [1-1-91]

verses.sanskrit: न च अग्निजम् भयम् किन्चित् न अप्सु मज्जन्ति जन्तवः ।न वातजम् भयम् किन्चित् न अपि ज्वर कृतम् तथा ॥१-१-९२॥
verses.text:
"In the kingdom of Rama, there will be no fear for his subjects from wildfires, gale-storms or from diseases, 
and there will be no fear from hunger or thieves, nor the cattle will be drowned in floodwaters as well... [1-1-92, 
93a]

verses.sanskrit: न च अपि क्षुत् भयम् तत्र न तस्कर भयम् तथा ।नगराणि च राष्ट्राणि धन धान्य युतानि च ॥१-१-९३॥
verses.text:
"Cities and states as well will be replete with wealth and grains likewise. All his subjects will be highly glad always,
like in Krita yuga... [1-1-93b, 94a]

verses.sanskrit: नित्यम् प्रमुदिताः सर्वे यथा कृत युगे तथा ।अश्वमेध शतैः इष्ट्वा तथा बहु सुवर्णकैः ॥१-१-९४॥
verses.text:
"Like-wise the highly illustrious Rama having performed hundreds of horse rituals and having donated plentiful gold and crores
of cows and wealth that cannot be counted to Brahmanas and to scholars according to custom... [1-1-94b, 95a]

verses.sanskrit: गवाम् कोट्ययुतम् दत्त्वा विद्वभ्यो विधि पूर्वकम् ।असंख्येयम् धनम् दत्त्वा ब्राह्मणेभो महायशाः ॥१-१-९५॥
राज वंशान् शत गुणान् स्थाप इष्यति राघवः ।चातुर् वर्ण्यम् च लोके अस्मिन् स्वे स्वे धर्मे नियोक्ष्यति ॥१-१-९६॥
verses.text:
"In this world Raghava will establish kingly dynasties in hundredfold and he will be maintaining the 
four-caste system positing each in his own duty, may it be caste-bound or provincial-kingdom-bound 
duty, in order to achieve a perfect social harmony... [1-1-96]

verses.sanskrit: दश वर्ष सहस्राणि दश वर्ष शतानि च ।रामो राज्यम् उपासित्वा ब्रह्म लोकम् प्रयास्यति ॥१-१-९७॥
verses.text:
"Having served the kingdom for ten thousand years and another one thousand years, i.e. for a total 
of eleven thousand years, Rama will go to the abode of Brahma... [1-1-97]

verses.sanskrit: इदम् पवित्रम् पापघ्नम् पुण्यम् वेदैः च संमितम् ।यः पठेत् राम चरितम् सर्व पापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥१-१-९८॥
verses.text:
"This Ramayana is holy, sin-eradicating, merit-endowing, and conformable with the teachings of Vedas.
Whoever reads this Legend of Rama, will be verily liberated of all sins... [1-1-98]

verses.sanskrit: एतत् आख्यानम् आयुष्यम् पठन् रामायणम् नरः ।स पुत्र पौत्रः स गणः प्रेत्य स्वर्गे महीयते ॥१-१-९९॥
verses.text:
"This Ramayana is long-life giving. A narrative by reading which a human will be blessed with sons and grandsons, and after enjoying worldly
comforts with attendants, will be adored in heaven after demise... 
[1-1-99]

verses.sanskrit: पठन् द्विजो वाक् ऋषभत्वम् ईयात् ।स्यात् क्षत्रियो भूमि पतित्वम् ईयात् ॥वणिक् जनः पण्य फलत्वम् ईयात् ।जनः च शूद्रो अपि महत्त्वम् ईयात् ॥१-१-१००॥
verses.text:
"By reading this Ramayana, that person if a Brahmana obtains excellency in speech, if a Kshatriya obtains land-lordship, if Vaisya
attains profit in trade and even if a Sudra attains excellence." Thus Sage Narada gave a gist of Ramayana 
to Sage-poet Valmiki. [1-1-100] In the first chapter of Bala Kanda the gist of Ramayana is imbibed and it is called saMkShepa raamaayaNa, or also called baala raamaayaNa . The youngsters are asked to recite these stanzas daily for 
longevity and a perfect personality like that of Rama. The summarised stanzas reflected here are from 
the main epic. The canto-wise summarisation is as below: Valmiki composed Ramayana according to the letters of Gayatri Hymn and they are identified with the 
following verses in all cantos:
